summary: Reviewed the visible remediation spec for workflow and failure-path risks in private bookings. The spec correctly moves several checks server-side, but some flows still appear split across separate mutations, which risks UI-complete workflows missing side effects. Several lifecycle and payment concerns require verification because the review pack contains no changed files and the spec body is truncated.
appears_sound: D4 adds server-side transition validation inside `updateBooking()` instead of relying only on the edit-page dropdown, which addresses stale or forged form submissions in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:40`.

D1 sets `hold_expiry` to `null` for Date-TBD bookings, which directly prevents the expire-holds cron from cancelling records created with a placeholder date in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:160`.
D13 extends the deposit guard from cancelled-only to cancelled-or-completed bookings, covering both manual and PayPal deposit workflows in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:130`.

findings.description: A staff user can change status through the edit form; the spec validates the transition but still lets `updateBooking()` apply `status` directly instead of routing through the canonical status-change workflow. Failure sequence: user edits a confirmed booking to cancelled, the UI shows cancellation complete, but cancellation side effects such as SMS cleanup, payment-state handling, or revalidation can be skipped.
findings.evidence: D4 says the edit page posts status through `updateBooking()` and the fix only adds validation/dropdown filtering, while Group F separately lists SMS and notification cancellation work in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:20`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: `updateBookingStatus()` may only perform validation today, or the omitted Group F section may refactor side effects into shared helpers used by both paths.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the full spec or implementation to verify every status change path calls one shared transition function that performs validation plus all cancellation/completion side effects.

findings.description: The Date-TBD SMS fix contradicts its own requirement by checking only `internal_notes` instead of the actual `date_tbd` state. Failure sequence: a TBD booking lacks or later loses the note marker, creation SMS formats the placeholder `event_date`, and the customer receives a false concrete date.
findings.evidence: The prose says to use Date-TBD when the booking has `date_tbd` or `DATE_TBD_NOTE`, but the code sets `isTbd` only from `booking.internal_notes?.includes(DATE_TBD_NOTE)` in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:182`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The booking object passed to `sendCreationSms()` may not include `date_tbd`, and the note marker may be guaranteed by `createBooking()`.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check the `sendCreationSms()` argument type and creation/update paths to confirm whether `date_tbd` is available and whether notes can ever omit the marker.

findings.description: The Date-TBD creation branch says there should be no balance due date, but the proposed assignment preserves any submitted `input.balance_due_date`. Failure sequence: a stale form or direct action submits `date_tbd=true` with a balance due date, reminders or balance workflows can fire before a real event date exists.
findings.evidence: The D1 code initializes `balanceDueDate` from input, then in the TBD branch uses `balanceDueDate = balanceDueDate || null` rather than forcing it to `null` in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:165`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The UI may never submit `balance_due_date` for TBD bookings, or staff may intentionally set a manual due date even when the event date is unknown.
findings.what_would_confirm: Verify form payloads and balance reminder cron behavior, then decide whether TBD bookings must hard-null `balance_due_date` server-side.

findings.description: The completed-booking deposit fix appears to be a pre-operation status guard only, which may not protect PayPal capture from concurrent status changes or partial completion. Failure sequence: deposit capture starts while the booking is draft, another action completes/cancels it, PayPal capture succeeds, and the local record then either records an invalid deposit or fails after money moved.
findings.evidence: D13 instructs only changing the status guard at `payments.ts:324`, while the acceptance criteria explicitly include PayPal deposit capture in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:134`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The omitted Group J transaction-safety section may add a post-capture recheck, idempotency key, or conditional update around the payment record.
findings.what_would_confirm: Review the full D15-D17 transaction-safety spec and `payments.ts` to verify there is an atomic or compensating path for external capture followed by local persistence.

findings.description: The visible Date-TBD fix covers creation but not the later transition from TBD to a real date. Failure sequence: staff confirms the event date, but `hold_expiry` and `balance_due_date` remain `null`, so holds never expire and balance workflows never run for that booking.
findings.evidence: The visible D1 section defines the create-time TBD branch and creation SMS wording, but the pack is truncated before any update lifecycle or acceptance criteria for clearing TBD state in `tasks/private-bookings-review/remediation-spec.md:160`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The omitted portion of the 953-line spec may define `updateBooking()` behavior for setting a real date and recomputing derived dates.
findings.what_would_confirm: Check the full D1 section for an explicit TBD-to-dated transition, including recomputing hold expiry, balance due date, notes, and SMS copy.

empty_categories: No UI-only status validation found in the visible D4 plan; server-side validation is explicitly required.
No immediate expire-holds failure for newly created TBD bookings is visible after the proposed `hold_expiry = null` change.
No frontend unmount, async React state, or empty-array rendering risks are present in the supplied pack excerpt.
